findings: grep -F 'spec-continuation-of' commands/spec-update.md returns zero matches — producer absence confirmed by direct file read
commit.md:164 confirmed to contain exactly: grep -lF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} -->' candidate.md as the ONLY content predicate
grep -rF 'spec-continuation-of' docs/dev/specs/ returns zero matches across all 4 existing spec files
BA diagnosis_completeness: 'incomplete (inferred only)' is an honest self-assessment; the inferences are validated by QA's direct file reads and grep results
evidence_type: 'inferred' is appropriate — the gap is structural (missing instruction text) not a runtime failure

User requirement verbatim: 'commands/spec-update.md continuation-spec mode never writes the <!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} --> marker required by commands/commit.md Step 7 Stage 3. Fix the producer.'
BA scope: only commands/spec-update.md. Consumer (commit.md) explicitly excluded as Won't Have. Correct.
BA Won't Have: retroactive injection into existing 4 spec files. User requirement says 'Fix the producer' — no universal claim over existing files. Scope exclusion is justified.
BA Won't Have: no change to commit.md, dev.md, close.md, dev-command.md. These route through spec-update; single fix-point is sound.
No universal scope claim ('all', 'every') in user requirement that BA's Won't Have silently omits.
5 ACs cover the full scope of the requirement: marker written (AC1), content predicate passes (AC2), no literal placeholder (AC3), mode isolation (AC4), idempotency (AC5).
Requirement is 'fix the producer' — investigation is structural text inspection, not runtime tracing. BA completed what was required.
BA ran grep -rF 'spec-continuation-of' docs/dev/specs/ and confirmed zero matches — the measured gap claim is reproducible.
BA read commands/spec-update.md lines 50-81 and confirmed absence of marker-writing instruction — root cause is confirmed.
BA traced root_cause_commit to aea75d9 and provided timeline — investigation is complete for a MICRO structural gap.
commands/spec-update.md exists at /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/commands/spec-update.md — verified by Read
Lines 50-81 of spec-update.md are the continuation-spec mode cycle-block appending instructions — confirmed to contain no marker-writing instruction
commands/commit.md line 164 is confirmed as the consumer grep predicate — exact string matches BA's description
No spurious files listed in affected_files or component_chain
AC1 check: 'grep -F "<!-- spec-continuation-of: T -->" <spec_path>' — consistent between ticket-*.md AC text and acceptance-criteria-*.json check.command. No drift.
AC2 check: 'grep -lF "<!-- spec-continuation-of: T -->" <spec_path>' — consistent. No drift.
AC3 checks: two negative greps — consistent between BA spec and ACs JSON. No drift.

AC4 check: 'grep -n "spec-continuation-of" commands/spec-update.md' with human line-range verification — this is the only AC that requires judgment rather than a pure exit-code check. The AC text correctly describes the intent. This is a known limitation of a mode-isolation check without awk/Python tooling, but the literal AC text and the check.command are consistent with each other.
AC5 check: 'grep -c "spec-continuation-of: T" <spec_path>' expected_value '1' — consistent. Codex flags that grep -c counts lines not occurrences. See codex_findings_classified below.
No case where the AC literal text requires verifier recipe X but the only runnable check is recipe Y.

verified_claims.evidence: commit.md:164 text: 'run grep -lF "<!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} -->" candidate.md — this is the ONLY content predicate allowed; no other grep, no free-form content scan'

verified_claims.claim: spec-update.md continuation-spec mode does NOT currently write this marker
verified_claims.evidence: grep -F 'spec-continuation-of' commands/spec-update.md returns zero matches. Lines 50-81 read in full — cycle-block appending instructions present but no marker instruction.

verified_claims.claim: Fix (add marker injection instruction to spec-update.md) is sufficient
verified_claims.evidence: commit.md Step 7 Stage 3 dispatches an inline Agent with instructions 'Follow the ## Continuation-spec mode instructions from /root/.claude/commands/spec-update.md exactly' (commit.md:188). Adding the marker instruction to spec-update.md ## Continuation-spec mode will be consumed by this dispatch path. Other commands (dev.md, dev-command.md, close.md) invoke /spec-update as a slash-command, inheriting the updated instructions.

verified_claims.claim: ACs correctly test the fix via grep exit 0
verified_claims.evidence: AC1: grep -F for literal marker; AC2: grep -lF matching commit.md Stage 3 predicate exactly; AC3: two negative greps; AC4: line-range placement check; AC5: grep -c idempotency. All consistent with the requirement.

verified_claims.claim: Scope is MICRO and only commands/spec-update.md needs to change
verified_claims.evidence: grep -rF 'spec-continuation-of' across entire repo: only commit.md:164 (consumer, correct), close-report and dev-report references (documentation). commands/spec-update.md is the sole producer that requires a new instruction.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 6 findings: (1) AC5 grep -c counts lines not occurrences; (2) multi-task cardinality not AC-tested; (3) new-spec creation path not explicit in AC1; (4) routing confidence in 'other commands route through spec-update'; (5) no-task-id behavior not covered; (6) AC4 manual line-range check is weak.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Findings 1-3 and 5-6 classified as in_scope_minor observations that QA flags to BA as recommended AC improvements but do NOT block APPROVED verdict for a MICRO instruction-only cycle. Finding 4 classified out_of_scope — commit.md:188 explicitly states 'Follow the ## Continuation-spec mode instructions from /root/.claude/commands/spec-update.md exactly', confirming routing.

codex_findings_classified.summary: AC5 uses grep -c which counts lines not occurrences; two markers on one line would still pass
codex_findings_classified.rationale: Real imprecision in the AC5 check. However for a markdown command file the probability of two markers on one line is essentially zero. Recommended improvement: use 'grep -oF "<!-- spec-continuation-of: T -->" <spec_path> | wc -l' for exact occurrence count.

codex_findings_classified.summary: No AC tests multi-task cardinality: spec with marker U should still get marker T added when T != U
codex_findings_classified.rationale: BA prose (Edge Cases, Must Have) covers 'once per (task-id, spec file) pair' conceptually, and the 'if the marker for that task-id is already present, do not write a second copy' idempotency check logically requires per-task-id scoping. But no dedicated AC tests that a new T marker is written when U marker already exists. Recommended addition: AC6 for multi-task scenario.

codex_findings_classified.summary: AC1 says 'when agent appends a new cycle block' but spec-update can also create a new spec from template — create path may be missed
codex_findings_classified.rationale: The BA spec mentions 'At line 1 for a newly created spec' in the Must Have requirements and Technical Hints, but AC1 WHEN clause says 'appends a new cycle block' which could be read as only covering the update path. Recommended: amend AC1 WHEN clause to cover both create and update paths explicitly.

codex_findings_classified.summary: Scope claim that dev.md, close.md, dev-command.md route through spec-update is overconfident
codex_findings_classified.rationale: QA verified: commit.md:188 explicitly says 'Follow the ## Continuation-spec mode instructions from /root/.claude/commands/spec-update.md exactly' — the inline Agent dispatch is the path where Stage 3 candidate was found, which is the only path where Stage 3 can fire. Other commands invoke /spec-update as a slash-command, which loads the updated spec-update.md instructions. The single-fix-point analysis is sound. This finding is out of scope for the MICRO cycle.

codex_findings_classified.summary: No AC or instruction covers behavior when task-id is unavailable — agent might write 'unknown' or empty marker
codex_findings_classified.rationale: AC3 only covers two specific literal placeholder forms (${TASK_ID} and <task-id>). If task-id is genuinely unknown, an agent might write 'unknown' or skip the marker silently. The BA should add guidance: 'Write the marker only when a non-empty resolved task-id is available; if task-id is unknown, omit the marker and log a warning — do NOT write a placeholder.'

codex_findings_classified.summary: AC4 manual line-range check is non-deterministic for automated runners
codex_findings_classified.rationale: QA already flagged this in the draft. Codex proposes replacing with awk/Python. The AC text is internally consistent (grep -n + human line-range check) but could be tightened. For a BA-validation cycle this is acceptable; for a dev-execution cycle a more deterministic check would be better.

issues.finding: AC5 check 'grep -c' counts matching lines not occurrences; also the pattern 'spec-continuation-of: T' is not the full marker string '<!-- spec-continuation-of: T -->', so a prose line mentioning the pattern could produce a false negative
issues.recommendation: Replace AC5 check with: grep -oF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: T -->' <spec_path> | wc -l must equal 1

issues.finding: No AC tests multi-task cardinality: when a spec already has marker for task U and spec-update runs for task T != U, both markers should exist. A naive implementation that checks 'any marker present' and skips would pass AC5 but fail this scenario.
issues.recommendation: Add AC6: given spec contains <!-- spec-continuation-of: U -->, when spec-update runs for T != U, then both markers present exactly once.

issues.finding: AC1 WHEN clause 'agent appends a new cycle block' does not explicitly cover the spec-create path (spec-update.md:35-39). BA Must Have includes the create path ('at line 1 for a newly created spec') but the AC WHEN is ambiguous.
issues.recommendation: Amend AC1 WHEN to 'agent creates a new spec file OR appends a cycle block to an existing spec file at docs/dev/specs/spec-*.md'.

issues.finding: No explicit handling in the spec for the case where task-id is unavailable at spec-update invocation time. AC3 covers two literal placeholder forms but not 'unknown', empty string, or silent omission.
issues.recommendation: Add instruction guidance and AC clause: if task-id is not resolvable, omit the marker and emit a warning; never write a non-resolved value.

qa.approval_notes: The BA spec for task 20260524-125300-C is APPROVED. All 5 BA-validation dimensions pass. The core facts are verified: (1) commit.md:164 requires the exact marker '<!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} -->'; (2) commands/spec-update.md currently has zero occurrences of 'spec-continuation-of' — the producer gap is confirmed; (3) the fix (add the marker instruction to ## Continuation-spec mode) is sufficient because commit.md:188 dispatches an inline Agent with 'Follow the ## Continuation-spec mode instructions from /root/.claude/commands/spec-update.md exactly'; (4) the 5 ACs are well-structured and testable; (5) scope is correctly MICRO with only commands/spec-update.md in scope. Four minor issues are flagged for BA to consider improving in AC precision (AC5 grep-c, multi-task cardinality AC, AC1 create-path coverage, no-task-id guard), but none blocks the fix from being correct and sufficient. The Codex finding about routing confidence (finding 4) is out of scope — commit.md:188 proves the single-fix-point analysis is sound.
